title: pittosporum paniculatum
intro: Pittosporum paniculatum, also known as the Toonga or New Zealand tobacco tree, is a plant species native to New Zealand and Australia, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and dense, evergreen foliage. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Pittosporum paniculatum include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Pittosporum paniculatum include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
